key_insight: MOSAIC is an agentic multi-LLM framework that can synthesize structured EHR data into multidimensional disease-severity phenotypes beyond rule-based scoring.
clinical_relevance: In type 2 diabetes, the system may help stratify patients by severity and risk using information that conventional algorithms often miss.
method: Two-phase agentic LLM pipeline for severity phenotyping
Tested on synthetic EHR cohorts for type 2 diabetes
Compared with three algorithmic severity standards and survival/complication outcomes
evidence_signal: Open-weight MOSAIC closely matched the proprietary version (weighted kappa = 0.773) and showed moderate-to-fair agreement with established severity scores while separating mortality and complication outcomes (log-rank p < 0.001).
watch_item: Findings were evaluated in synthetic data and in one disease area, so real-world generalization, safety, and calibration still need validation.
paper_title: Multi-Large Language Model Orchestrated Severity Assessment of Clinical Records (MOSAIC)
